entities.name: OCCUPATIONAL STRESS FACTORS
entities.description: Factors causing stress in the workplace including shift work, working hours, job demand, job control, social support, job insecurity, and salary rewards

entities.name: EMPLOYEE PERFORMANCE
entities.description: The dependent variable measuring the effectiveness and output of employees at the workplace

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL RESEARCH SECTOR EMPLOYEES
entities.description: The human subjects of the study working in the agricultural research sector

entities.name: HYDERABAD METRO, INDIA
entities.description: The geographical area where the study was conducted

entities.name: SHIFT WORK
entities.description: An occupational stress factor involving non-standard work shifts

entities.name: WORKING HOURS
entities.description: An occupational stress factor relating to the number of hours worked per week

entities.name: HIGH ON JOB DEMAND
entities.description: An occupational stress factor characterized by excessive work requirements

entities.name: LACK OF JOB CONTROL
entities.description: An occupational stress factor where employees have little influence over their work

entities.name: SOCIAL SUPPORT
entities.description: An occupational stress factor relating to the support received from colleagues and supervisors

entities.name: JOB INSECURITY
entities.description: An occupational stress factor involving the fear of losing one's job

entities.name: LACK OF SALARY REWARDS
entities.description: An occupational stress factor relating to insufficient financial compensation

entities.name: SURVEY
entities.description: The overall research approach used to collect data from 756 employees

entities.name: STRUCTURED SELF-REPORTED QUESTIONNAIRE
entities.description: The instrument used to collect data on general characteristics, health, and job characteristics

entities.name: 5-POINT LIKERT TYPE SCALE
entities.description: The measurement scale used to quantify occupational stress factors

entities.name: CHI-SQUARE TEST
entities.description: A statistical test used to observe associations between stress factors and performance

entities.name: MULTIVARIATE LOGISTIC REGRESSION ANALYSIS
entities.description: A statistical analysis used to measure the association between multiple independent stress factors and employee performance

entities.name: CRONBACH’S ALPHA
entities.description: A reliability statistic used to measure the internal consistency of the study instrument
